up.method: HPLC (RI detector); substrate consumption rate, Table 3

growth_records.cite: Balazs M et al. Organic Acid Production by Basfia succiniciproducens from Agro-Industrial By-Products. BioTech (Basel) (2025). DOI:10.3390/biotech14030068. [PMC12452410]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: 15 g/L Fructose - Substrate consumption 0.610 g/Lh; Specific growth rate 0.183 h-1; Succinic acid 2.433 g/L; Formic 0.292; Acetic 1.217; Lactic 0.301; Succinic acid yield 0.141 g/g consumed sugar.
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch, 37C, pH7, 1xM9 + 15 g/L fructose. mu=0.183 h-1 (reported, OD600 exponential 0-3 h); td=ln2/0.183=3.788 h. Substrate uptake 0.610 g/L/h (volumetric). Product titers at 12 h from Table 3. Succinic yield 0.141 g/g consumed sugar. M9 1x values computed from stated 5x macro stock (Na2HPO4 17.7/5=3.54, KH2PO4 17/5=3.4, NH4Cl 10/5=2.0, NaCl 2.5/5=0.5 g/L).

growth_records.cite: Balazs M et al. Organic Acid Production by Basfia succiniciproducens from Agro-Industrial By-Products. BioTech (Basel) (2025). DOI:10.3390/biotech14030068. [PMC12452410]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: 55 g/L Fructose - Substrate consumption 2.244 g/Lh; Specific growth rate 0.421 h-1; Succinic 4.811 g/L; Formic 0.610; Acetic 0.779; Lactic 1.947; Succinic yield 0.072 g/g.
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch, 37C, pH7, 1xM9 + 55 g/L fructose. mu=0.421 h-1 (reported); td=ln2/0.421=1.646 h. Substrate uptake 2.244 g/L/h. Product titers at 12 h (Table 3). Succinic yield 0.072 g/g consumed sugar (lower yield at high sugar, more lactate).

growth_records.cite: Balazs M et al. Organic Acid Production by Basfia succiniciproducens from Agro-Industrial By-Products. BioTech (Basel) (2025). DOI:10.3390/biotech14030068. [PMC12452410]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: 27 g/L Lactose - Substrate consumption 0.700 g/Lh; Specific growth rate 0.308 h-1; Succinic 4.334 g/L; Formic 0.500; Acetic 0.950; Lactic 2.319; Succinic yield 0.276 g/g. 'lactose-containing mediums resulted in the highest succinic
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch, 37C, pH7, 1xM9 + 27 g/L lactose (control for whey). mu=0.308 h-1 (reported); td=ln2/0.308=2.250 h. Lactose uptake 0.700 g/L/h. Product titers at 12 h. Highest defined-sugar succinic yield 0.276 g/g lactose. Lactic acid is the major by-product here.

growth_records.cite: Balazs M et al. Organic Acid Production by Basfia succiniciproducens from Agro-Industrial By-Products. BioTech (Basel) (2025). DOI:10.3390/biotech14030068. [PMC12452410]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: Apple Pomace (W) - Substrate consumption 0.610 g/Lh; Specific growth rate 0.152 h-1; Succinic 1.637 g/L; Formic 0.443; Acetic 0.779; Lactic 0.829; Succinic yield 0.224 g/g; Overall yield 0.038 g succinic/g apple pomace.
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch on agro by-product, 37C, pH7, apple pomace:M9 1:1. mu=0.152 h-1 (reported, lowest); td=ln2/0.152=4.560 h. Sugar uptake 0.610 g/L/h. Succinic yield 0.224 g/g consumed sugar (similar to 0.22 g/g on Arundo donax hydrolysate). Overall 0.038 g succinic/g raw apple pomace. First report of succinic acid from apple pomace by this species.

growth_records.cite: Balazs M et al. Organic Acid Production by Basfia succiniciproducens from Agro-Industrial By-Products. BioTech (Basel) (2025). DOI:10.3390/biotech14030068. [PMC12452410]
growth_records.snippet: the whey-based medium proved to be superior in terms of substrate consumption, maximal biomass, and succinic acid yield as well, reaching 0.61 g/Lh of substrate utilization rate (lactose), and a 5.00 g/L of succinic acid concentration along
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch on cheese whey, 37C, pH7, whey:M9 1:1. mu=0.310 h-1 (reported); td=ln2/0.310=2.236 h. Lactose uptake 1.383 g/L/h (highest, Table 3). Highest succinic titer 5.007 g/L, yield 0.236 g/g consumed lactose; overall 0.008 g succinic/g whey. NOTE: narrative text cites substrate util 0.61 g/Lh for whey but Table 3 gives 1.383 g/Lh; Table 3 value used. Acetic acid unusually high (3.573 g/L); paper notes acetate may act as co-substrate. First report of succinic acid from cheese whey by this species.

growth_records.strain: wild type DSM-22022 (isolated from Holstein cow rumen)

up.method: FBA constraint fixed from literature (not measured in this study)

mfa.note: Fixed from literature for FBA, NOT measured here

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: succinic acid production (in silico FBA prediction)
mfa.note: In-silico xylose gave best predicted succinate 5.79 mmol/gDW/h; glucose value not separately stated

yields.Yxs: 0.54 g biomass/L final (biomass concentration, not per-substrate yield)

growth_records.cite: Balázs, Márta et al. Substrate type and CO2 addition significantly influence succinic acid production of Basfia succiniciproducens. Biotechnology Letters (2023). DOI:10.1007/s10529-023-03406-7. [PMC10432361]
growth_records.snippet: the highest value was 0.739 h−1 for glucose in the CO2 gas phase... applying only CO2 resulted in a higher (0.54 g biomass L−1) cell density... highest succinic acid yield was obtained on glucose (0.437 mM·mM−1)... acetic acid on glucose wa
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor (Sartorius Biostat A Plus), M9 medium, 20 g/L glucose, 37 C, pH 7.0 (NaOH/HCl), 150 rpm, duplicates, 12 h, anaerobic CO2 sparge 60 mL/min. EXPERIMENTAL: mu = 0.739 h-1 (Fig.5 'G', growth rate on exponential phase); td = ln2/0.739 = 0.938 h (calculated). Final biomass 0.54 g/L. Product yields are molar (mM product / mM substrate): succinate 0.437, acetate 0.62, formate 0.581 - measured by HPLC. Absolute secretion rates (mmol/gDW/h) not given (only yields + concentration profiles in Fig.5). glucose uptake 7.7 mmol/gDW/h is a MODEL constraint fixed from literature (is_experimental:false), not measured. In-silico FBA best succinate flux 5.79 mmol/gDW/h was on XYLOSE. Organism facultative anaerobic capnophile.

up.method: FBA constraint fixed from literature (not measured)

product_yields.succinic_acid: up to 0.437 mol/mol (glucose best; gas-phase-specific value not separated)

growth_records.cite: Balázs, Márta et al. Substrate type and CO2 addition significantly influence succinic acid production of Basfia succiniciproducens. Biotechnology Letters (2023). DOI:10.1007/s10529-023-03406-7. [PMC10432361]
growth_records.snippet: In the case of glucose used as the sole carbon source, applying only CO2 resulted in a higher (0.54 g biomass L−1) cell density, compared to CO2/AIR conditions where a lower cell concentration (0.38 g biomass L−1) was observed.
growth_records.notes: Glucose under CO2/AIR (microaerobic): final biomass 0.38 g/L (measured, lower than CO2 anaerobic 0.54 g/L). Growth rate not separately reported for this gas phase ('similar value ... obtained for xylose and glycerol only with CO2/AIR'; the 0.739 h-1 max was glucose+CO2). Glucose uptake 7.7 mmol/gDW/h = literature model constraint, not measured. 37 C, pH 7, 150 rpm, 12 h.

up.method: FBA constraint fixed from literature (not measured)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: succinic acid production (in silico FBA prediction, xylose)
mfa.note: In-silico prediction: xylose was best predicted substrate for succinate (5.79 mmol/gDW/h)

growth_records.cite: Balázs, Márta et al. Substrate type and CO2 addition significantly influence succinic acid production of Basfia succiniciproducens. Biotechnology Letters (2023). DOI:10.1007/s10529-023-03406-7. [PMC10432361]
growth_records.snippet: higher acetic acid yields under anaerobic conditions (0.367 mM·mM−1) and 0.277 mM·mM−1 yields of succinic acid... in silico predictions... xylose was the best substrate for producing succinic acid (5.79 mmol·g−1·h−1)
growth_records.notes: Xylose under CO2 (anaerobic): measured molar yields acetate 0.367 mol/mol, succinate 0.277 mol/mol (HPLC). Final biomass 0.71 g/L (similar under both aeration modes). Growth rate for this condition not numerically given. xylose uptake 9.24 mmol/gDW/h = literature FBA constraint (not measured). In-silico succinate flux 5.79 mmol/gDW/h is a PREDICTION for xylose. 37 C, pH 7, 12 h.

up.method: FBA constraint fixed from literature (not measured)

product_yields.acetic_acid: produced in larger amounts under anaerobic phase (value not quantified)

growth_records.cite: Balázs, Márta et al. Substrate type and CO2 addition significantly influence succinic acid production of Basfia succiniciproducens. Biotechnology Letters (2023). DOI:10.1007/s10529-023-03406-7. [PMC10432361]
growth_records.snippet: Microaerobic conditions (CO2/AIR) seem to be more favorable for the strain in case of glycerol... (0.46 g biomass L−1)... compared to CO2 condition (0.16 g biomass L−1)... in the anaerobic phase, acetic acid was produced in larger amounts
growth_records.notes: Glycerol under CO2 (anaerobic): final biomass only 0.16 g/L (roughly a third of microaerobic). Acetate produced in larger amounts (not quantified). Growth rate not numerically given for this condition. glycerol uptake 15.4 mmol/gDW/h = literature FBA constraint (not measured). 37 C, pH 7, 12 h.
